<content>
1: ---
2: title: Accordion
3: description: Animated accordion with spring physics, group morphing, and accessible ARIA attributes.
4: ---
5: 
6: import { BouncyAccordionPreview } from "@/demo/accordion-demo";
7: 
8: <ComponentPreview name="accordion">
9:   <BouncyAccordionPreview />
10: </ComponentPreview>
11: 
12: ## Installation
13: 
14: If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:
15: 
16: <CodeBlockCommand
17:   __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
18:   __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
19:   __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
20:   __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
21: />
22: 
23: Otherwise, use the full registry URL:
24: 
25: <CodeBlockCommand
26:   __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
27:   __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
28:   __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
29:   __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
30: />
31: 
32: ## Usage
33: 
34: After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:
35: 
36: ```tsx
37: import { Accordion, type AccordionItem } from "@/components/ui/accordion";
38: 
39: const items: AccordionItem[] = [
40:   {
41:     id: "item-1",
42:     title: "First question",
43:     description: "Answer to the first question.",
44:   },
45:   {
46:     id: "item-2",
47:     title: "Second question",
48:     description: "Answer to the second question.",
49:   },
50: ];
51: 
52: export function MyComponent() {
53:   return <Accordion items={items} defaultValue="item-1" />;
54: }
55: ```
56: 
57: ## Props
58: 
59: | Prop            | Type                              | Default | Description                                           |
60: | --------------- | --------------------------------- | ------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
61: | `items`         | `AccordionItem[]`                 | -       | Array of accordion items                              |
62: | `value`         | `string \| null`                  | -       | Controlled currently open item id                     |
63: | `defaultValue`  | `string \| null`                  | `null`  | Initially open item id                                |
64: | `onValueChange` | `(value: string \| null) => void` | -       | Callback fired when the open item changes             |
65: | `collapsible`   | `boolean`                         | `true`  | When false, an open item cannot be closed by clicking |
66: | `className`     | `string`                          | -       | Classes applied to the root wrapper                   |
67: | `classNames`    | `AccordionClassNames`             | -       | Override classes for individual sub-elements          |
68: 
69: ### AccordionItem
70: 
71: | Field         | Type        | Description                              |
72: | ------------- | ----------- | ---------------------------------------- |
73: | `id`          | `string`    | Unique identifier for the item           |
74: | `title`       | `ReactNode` | Content shown in the trigger row         |
75: | `description` | `ReactNode` | Content revealed when the item is open   |
76: | `icon`        | `ReactNode` | Optional icon displayed before the title |
77: | `disabled`    | `boolean`   | Disables the item                        |
78: 
79: ### AccordionClassNames
80: 
81: | Key           | Description                  |
82: | ------------- | ---------------------------- |
83: | `root`        | Wrapper around all items     |
84: | `item`        | Each accordion row container |
85: | `trigger`     | The clickable button row     |
86: | `icon`        | The icon wrapper span        |
87: | `title`       | The title text span          |
88: | `chevron`     | The chevron rotation wrapper |
89: | `content`     | The animated content wrapper |
90: | `description` | The description text div     |

(End of file - total 90 lines)
</content>

import { BouncyAccordionPreview } from "@/demo/accordion-demo";

<ComponentPreview name="accordion">
  <BouncyAccordionPreview />
</ComponentPreview>

## Installation

If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"

display.text:
---
title: Accordion
description: Animated accordion with spring physics, group morphing, and accessible ARIA attributes.
---

import { BouncyAccordionPreview } from "@/demo/accordion-demo";

<ComponentPreview name="accordion">
  <BouncyAccordionPreview />
</ComponentPreview>

## Installation

If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
/>

Otherwise, use the full registry URL:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
/>

## Usage

After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:

```tsx
import { Accordion, type AccordionItem } from "@/components/ui/accordion";

const items: AccordionItem[] = [
  {
    id: "item-1",
    title: "First question",
    description: "Answer to the first question.",
  },
  {
    id: "item-2",
    title: "Second question",
    description: "Answer to the second question.",
  },
];

export function MyComponent() {
  return <Accordion items={items} defaultValue="item-1" />;
}
```

## Props

| Prop            | Type                              | Default | Description                                           |
| --------------- | --------------------------------- | ------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `items`         | `AccordionItem[]`                 | -       | Array of accordion items                              |
| `value`         | `string \| null`                  | -       | Controlled currently open item id                     |
| `defaultValue`  | `string \| null`                  | `null`  | Initially open item id                                |
| `onValueChange` | `(value: string \| null) => void` | -       | Callback fired when the open item changes             |
| `collapsible`   | `boolean`                         | `true`  | When false, an open item cannot be closed by clicking |
| `className`     | `string`                          | -       | Classes applied to the root wrapper                   |
| `classNames`    | `AccordionClassNames`             | -       | Override classes for individual sub-elements          |

### AccordionItem

| Field         | Type        | Description                              |
| ------------- | ----------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `id`          | `string`    | Unique identifier for the item           |
| `title`       | `ReactNode` | Content shown in the trigger row         |
| `description` | `ReactNode` | Content revealed when the item is open   |
| `icon`        | `ReactNode` | Optional icon displayed before the title |
| `disabled`    | `boolean`   | Disables the item                        |

### AccordionClassNames

| Key           | Description                  |
| ------------- | ---------------------------- |
| `root`        | Wrapper around all items     |
| `item`        | Each accordion row container |
| `trigger`     | The clickable button row     |
| `icon`        | The icon wrapper span        |
| `title`       | The title text span          |
| `chevron`     | The chevron rotation wrapper |
| `content`     | The animated content wrapper |
| `description` | The description text div     |

metadata.preview:
prsui

A shadcn registry of polished, ready-to-install components by Pranjal Mandavkar. Includes documentation, landing page, and everything you need to browse and install components.

## Features - 📦 **Copy/paste ownership** - Install components and own the code - 📚 **Documentation site** - Beautiful docs powered by Fumadocs - 🎨 **Shadcn registry compatible** - Works with `npx shadcn add`

display.text:
prsui

A shadcn registry of polished, ready-to-install components by Pranjal Mandavkar. Includes documentation, landing page, and everything you need to browse and install components.

## Features - 📦 **Copy/paste ownership** - Install components and own the code - 📚 **Documentation site** - Beautiful docs powered by Fumadocs - 🎨 **Shadcn registry compatible** - Works with `npx shadcn add` - 🤖 **[Agent ready](https://www.mintlify.com/score/startercn)** - Includes `llms.txt`, `llms-full.txt`, agent skills discovery routes, and API catalog endpoints - 🔊 **[Web audio feedback](https://audio.raphaelsalaja.com/)** - Built-in sound effects powered by `@web-kits/audio` - 📳 **[Web haptics](https://haptics.lochie.me/)** - Optional haptic feedback hooks for supported devices via `web-haptics` - ✨ **[Motion animations](https://motion.dev/)** - `motion`-powered UI polish for copy states, text transitions, and interactive elements - 🎯 **[Animated icons](https://lucide-animated.com/)** - Reusable animated icons for navigation, sharing, sponsorship, and CTAs - 🔄 **[View transitions](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/config/next-config-js/viewTransition)** - Next.js view transitions enabled for smoother navigation between pages - 🚀 **Deploy ready** - Deploy anywhere ## Built In - `Next.js 16` with the App Router - `React 19` and `TypeScript` - `Tailwind CSS 4` - `Fumadocs` for documentation - `shiki` + `rehype-pretty-code` for code blocks - `sonner` for toasts - `radix-ui` + `vaul` for accessible primitives - `@vercel/analytics` for analytics ## Usage Install any component with: ```bash npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/smooth-input.json ``` ## Project Structure ``` ├── registry/ │ └── new-york/ # Your components go here │ └── smooth-input.tsx ├── registry.json # Component registry manifest ├── content/docs/ # Documentation (MDX) ├── app/ # Next.js app └── public/r/ # Built registry files (auto-generated) ``` ## Scripts - `pnpm dev` - Start development server - `pnpm build` - Build for production - `pnpm registry:build` - Rebuild the component registry ## License [MIT](./LICENSE)

state.title: lib/github.ts

parts.text: Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me summarize my findings.

Here are the findings:

---

## Files Found Related to "github-stars"

### 1. Registry Component (shadcn-style distributable)

**`/home/himmel/personal/prsui/registry/new-york/github-stars.tsx`**

This is the distributable registry component. It is an **async server component** that fetches a GitHub repository's star count via the GitHub REST API (`https://api.github.com/repos/{repo}`), formats the count using `Intl.NumberFormat` with compact notation, and renders a button with the GitHub logo SVG, the formatted star count, and a `<Tooltip>` showing the full number. It supports `size` variants (`default`, `lg`, `sm`) via `class-variance-authority`, accepts a `repo` string prop (e.g. `"owner/repo"`), and has configurable `locales`.

---

### 2. Site-specific Component (internal use)

**`/home/himmel/personal/prsui/components/github-stars.tsx`**

This is a **client component** (`"use client"`) used internally by the prsui site itself (not the distributable registry component). It accepts a pre-fetched `stargazersCount` number, renders a ghost button linking to the prsui GitHub repo (via `LINK.GITHUB` and `UTM_PARAMS`), displays the star count in compact format, and shows the full count in a tooltip. It also plays a "success" sound effect via a `useFeedback` hook on click.

---

### 3. Wrapper Component

**`/home/himmel/personal/prsui/components/nav-item-github.tsx`**

An async server component that calls `getStargazerCount()` (from `@/lib/github`) and passes the result to the internal `<GitHubStars>` component. This is the navigation bar item that shows the project's live star count.

---

### 4. GitHub API Helper

**`/home/himmel/personal/prsui/lib/github.ts`**

Provides two cached functions: `getStargazers()` (fetches up to 500 individual stargazer profiles) and `getStargazerCount()` (fetches the repo's `stargazers_count`). Both use `unstable_cache` with a 24-hour revalidation. The repo is identified by `GITHUB.org` and `GITHUB.repo` constants.

---

## Files NOT Found (gaps in documentation/demos for github-stars)

### No MDX Documentation File

There is **no** `content/docs/components/github-stars.mdx` file. Every other registry component has a corresponding MDX doc file:

| Registry Component | MDX Doc |
|---|---|
| `accordion.tsx` | `content/docs/components/accordion.mdx` |
| `editable-chip.tsx` | `content/docs/components/editable-chip.mdx` |
| `inline-toast.tsx` | `content/docs/components/inline-toast.mdx` |
| `link.tsx` | `content/docs/components/link.mdx` |
| `logo-grid.tsx` | `content/docs/components/logo-grid.mdx` |
| `logo-marquee.tsx` | `content/docs/components/logo-marquee.mdx` |
| `split-action.tsx` | `content/docs/components/split-action.mdx` |
| `tooltip.tsx` | `content/docs/components/tooltip.mdx` |
| `typewriter.tsx` | `content/docs/components/typewriter.mdx` |
| `view-on-map.tsx` | `content/docs/components/view-on-map.mdx` |
| **`github-stars.tsx`** | **MISSING** |
| `smooth-input.tsx` | **MISSING** |

### No Demo File

There is **no** `demo/github-stars-demo.tsx` file. Every other registry component (except `smooth-input`) has a corresponding demo file in `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/demo/`, named `{component-name}-demo.tsx`.

---

## Registry Directory Structure Summary

The project follows a consistent three-part pattern for each distributable component:

```
registry/new-york/{component}.tsx    -- The actual component code (distributable)
content/docs/components/{component}.mdx -- MDX documentation with props table, usage, install commands
demo/{component}-demo.tsx            -- Interactive demo/preview imported by the MDX doc
```

The MDX files follow a standard structure:
1. YAML frontmatter with `title` and `description`
2. Import of the demo component from `@/demo/`
3. A `<ComponentPreview>` block wrapping the demo
4. Installation instructions with shadcn CLI commands (both short and full URL forms)
5. Usage code example
6. Props table

The `github-stars` component is missing both its MDX documentation file and its demo file compared to the established pattern. The `smooth-input` component is similarly missing both files.
